name: Nootropics & Focus
tagline: Sleep, water and sunlight first — supplements are the last 10%.

templates.title: Gentle Start Stack
templates.blurb: The evidence-first basics: hydration, omega-3, caffeine done right, and real wind-down.

routines.title: Morning stack
routines.footer: Add one supplement at a time and give it two weeks — and ask a pharmacist about interactions with any medication you take.

steps.label: Big glass of water before anything
steps.note: You wake up mildly dehydrated — fix that first

steps.label: Coffee + L-theanine
steps.note: Weekdays only — weekends keep tolerance low

steps.label: Ten-minute deep-work warmup
steps.note: One small task, no phone — momentum is the real nootropic

routines.title: Wind-down
routines.footer: Supplements aren't a substitute for sleep — the stack only works on top of 7+ hours.

templates.title: Deep Focus Days
templates.blurb: A weekday protocol built around protected focus blocks, with supplements in a supporting role.

routines.title: Focus protocol
routines.footer: If you're pregnant, or on any medication — especially for blood pressure, mood or sleep — check with a doctor before adding supplements.

steps.label: 90-minute deep work block
steps.note: Phone in another room; one task

questions.prompt: What are you hoping to improve?

options.title: Several cups, all day
options.subtitle: A schedule will help more than more caffeine

educationPages.title: Start low, go slow
educationPages.body: Add one thing at a time and hold it for two weeks before judging or adding more. Change three variables at once and you'll never know what helped — or what caused the headache.

educationPages.title: Sleep is the stack
educationPages.body: No supplement recovers the focus lost to a short night. If you're sleeping under seven hours, spend your effort there first — everything on top of good sleep works better.

educationPages.title: Interactions are real
educationPages.body: Supplements can interact with prescription medication — St. John's Wort famously breaks several. A pharmacist can check your exact combination in minutes, for free. This app can't, so please ask one.

educationPages.title: Evidence varies wildly
educationPages.body: Creatine and caffeine+theanine have solid research; many trendier compounds have almost none. Be suspicious of anything promising limitless effects, and buy from brands that publish third-party testing.

products.name: L-theanine
products.what: An amino acid from tea that smooths caffeine's edge.
products.why: Paired with coffee it keeps the alertness and drops most of the jitter — the best-evidenced beginner combo.
products.caution: Generally well tolerated. Check with a doctor if pregnant or on blood-pressure medication. Follow the label dose.

products.name: Magnesium glycinate
products.what: A gentle, well-absorbed form of magnesium.
products.why: Supports muscle relaxation and sleep quality; glycinate is the form least likely to upset your stomach.
products.order: With your wind-down, 30–60 minutes before bed.
products.caution: High doses can upset digestion — stay at the label dose. Ask a doctor first if you have kidney problems.

products.name: Omega-3 (EPA/DHA)
products.why: Broad, well-researched support for brain and heart health — a foundation, not a quick fix.
products.order: With breakfast — it absorbs with fat.
products.caution: Talk to a doctor before combining with blood thinners.

products.name: Creatine monohydrate
products.what: The most-studied sports supplement; plain monohydrate powder.
products.why: Strong evidence for physical performance and growing evidence for cognition, especially when sleep-deprived.
products.order: Any time of day, consistently — daily matters more than timing.
products.caution: Drink extra water. Ask a doctor first if you have kidney issues. Skip 'loading phases' — the label dose is enough.
